name: Green Power Smoothie
description: Spinach disappears into a creamy banana-mango base — you taste tropical fruit, not vegetables. One of the best gateway green smoothies for skeptics.
steps: Add almond milk and spinach to the blender first — liquid and greens at the bottom helps everything blend smooth.
Add frozen banana, mango, and ginger. Blend on high for 60 seconds until completely smooth.
Taste and add a small drizzle of honey if more sweetness is needed. Blend briefly and serve immediately.

name: Strawberry Banana Protein Smoothie
description: A thick, creamy post-workout smoothie built around Greek yogurt and vanilla protein powder. Tastes like a strawberry milkshake but actually keeps you full.
steps: Add all ingredients to the blender. Start on low, then increase to high.
Blend for 45–60 seconds until smooth and thick. If too thick to blend, add milk 1 tbsp at a time.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.

name: Tropical Mango Smoothie
description: Mango, pineapple, and coconut milk make this taste like a beach vacation in under 5 minutes. Bright, naturally sweet, and completely dairy-free.
steps: Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Blend on high for 60 seconds until completely smooth. Add more water or juice if too thick.
Divide between two glasses. Serve immediately with a lime wedge if desired.

name: Blueberry Açaí Smoothie
description: Deep purple, antioxidant-rich, and naturally sweet. Frozen açaí packs serious flavor and a smooth, sorbet-like texture without needing much else.
steps: Break the frozen açaí packet into chunks and add to the blender with all other ingredients.
Blend on high, starting slow and working up — açaí is thick. Stop and scrape the sides if needed.
Blend until smooth and pour into a glass. Drink right away for the best texture.
